title: FiRe 2026: AI Meets the University
snippet: With each passing day, AI tools are changing the nature of academia. Join us for a discussion of the complex opportunities AI presents to universities, researchers, and students; and of the ongoing endeavor to better perfect human knowledge and understanding. Speakers: Pradeep Khosla, Chancellor, UCSD.
match_reason: social (video) — Pradeep Khosla is the featured speaker in this YouTube video discussing the impact of AI on higher education.

title: Statement from UC Academic Senate on admissions review
snippet: The Academic Senate is not rescinding its commitment to a comprehensive review of standardized testing in admissions. Recognizing the significance of this issue, the Academic Senate is revising its timeline while ensuring the forthcoming review is thorough, evidence-based and informed by faculty expertise.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Ahmet Palazoglu is the University of California Academic Senate Chair who issued this official statement regarding the review of admissions policies, which are being reevaluated in part due to the widespread adoption of AI.

title: UC's admissions board abruptly suspends timeline to reconsider SAT and ACT requirements
snippet: In a statement Monday evening, UC Academic Senate Chair Ahmet Palazoglu said the senate was 'not rescinding its commitment to a comprehensive review of standardized testing in admissions.' ... A second group was to examine whether high school courses address 'widespread adoption of AI'.
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Ahmet Palazoglu is quoted directly as the UC Academic Senate Chair defending the ongoing, revised review of admissions policies impacted by AI-assisted applications.

title: A university investing in people
snippet: In welcoming attendees, Chancellor S. Jack Hu described UC Riverside's vision for expanding research, entrepreneurship and regional partnerships while continuing the university's longstanding commitment to opportunity and social mobility. 'The story of UC Riverside is not a story of excellence or access,' Hu said.
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Chancellor S. Jack Hu is quoted delivering the opening welcome address at UC Riverside's Science x AI Summit, which explored how emerging AI technologies are reshaping scientific discovery.

title: UCLA Awarded $300,000 State Grant to Launch Public Interest Technology Pathways
snippet: UCLA DataX and the UCLA Center on Resilience & Digital Justice have been awarded a two-year, $300,000 grant from the California Education Learning Lab to launch Public Interest Technology Pathways, a project that will expand access to public interest technology education and prepare students to address society's most pressing challenges through data, artificial intelligence and public-interest app
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Safiya Umoja Noble is the named Principal Investigator and lead of this newly highlighted state-funded AI and data education initiative at UCLA.

title: Preparing for heightened AI cybersecurity risks
snippet: A new report from the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine suggests artificial intelligence will fundamentally reshape the cybersecurity landscape... Giovanni Vigna, PhD, director of the AI Institute for Agent-based Cyber Threat Intelligence and Operation at the University of California, Santa Barbara, noted that 'cybersecurity will need to improve rapidly to meet this challen
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Giovanni Vigna is quoted directly in this article regarding his co-authored National Academies report on AI and cybersecurity.

title: Assembly Committee Adopts Resolution Urging Intersegmental Dialogue on AI in Higher Education
snippet: The Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted SCR 82, a resolution urging California's public higher education segments—the UC, CSU, and California Community Colleges—to convene a joint workgroup to coordinate on best practices for AI in instruction, student privacy, and campus services.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This legislative development directly impacts UC systemwide AI policy and mandates intersegmental collaboration on academic integrity, teaching, and privacy.

title: At the CSU, We See Access to Technology as an Equity Issue
snippet: In an op-ed, California State University leadership discusses their systemwide AI strategy to prevent an 'AI digital divide.' Highlighting a survey of 94,000 respondents, they argue that limiting access deepens inequity and makes it harder to teach the responsible and ethical use of AI.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — This item addresses AI access, equity, and systemwide strategy at a peer California public university system, which is highly relevant to the committee's charge.
